name: iavh_socialscience
displayName: Social Science and Humanities Metadata

name: name
displayName: description

name: iavh_unitOfAnalysis
displayName: Basic unit of analysis or observation that this Dataset describes, such as individuals, families/households, groups, institutions/organizations, administrative units, and more. For information about the DDI's controlled vocabulary for this element, please refer to the DDI web page at http://www.ddialliance.org/controlled-vocabularies.

name: iavh_universe
displayName: Description of the population covered by the data in the file; the group of people or other elements that are the object of the study and to which the study results refer. Age, nationality, and residence commonly help to  delineate a given universe, but any number of other factors may be used, such as age limits, sex, marital status, race, ethnic group, nationality, income, veteran status, criminal convictions, and more. The universe may consist of elements other than persons, such as housing units, court cases, deaths, countries, and so on. In general, it should be possible to tell from the description of the universe whether a given individual or element is a member of the population under study. Also known as the universe of interest, population of interest, and target population.

name: iavh_timeMethod
displayName: The time method or time dimension of the data collection, such as panel, cross-sectional, trend, time- series, or other.

name: iavh_timePeriodCovered
displayName: The time period that the data refer to. Also known as span. This is the time period covered by the data, not the dates of coding, collecting data, or making documents machine-readable

name: iavh_timePeriodCoveredStart
displayName: The start date of the time period that the data refer to

name: iavh_timePeriodCoveredEnd
displayName: The end date of the time period that the data refer to

name: iavh_dateOfCollection
displayName: The dates when the data were collected or generated

name: iavh_dateOfCollectionStart
displayName: The date when the data collection started

name: iavh_dateOfCollectionEnd
displayName: The date when the data collection ended

name: iavh_frequencyOfDataCollection
displayName: If the data collected includes more than one point in time, indicate the frequency with which the data was collected; that is, monthly, quarterly, or other.

name: iavh_dataCollector
displayName: Individual, agency or organization responsible for  administering the questionnaire or interview or compiling the data.

name: iavh_collectorTraining
displayName: Type of training provided to the data collector

name: iavh_samplingProcedure
displayName: Type of sample and sample design used to select the survey respondents to represent the population. May include reference to the target sample size and the sampling fraction.

name: iavh_targetSampleSize
displayName: Specific information regarding the target sample size, actual  sample size, and the formula used to determine this.

name: iavh_targetSampleActualSize
displayName: Actual sample size.

name: iavh_targetSampleSizeFormula
displayName: Formula used to determine target sample size.

name: iavh_deviationsFromSampleDesign
displayName: Show correspondence as well as discrepancies between the sampled units (obtained) and available statistics for the population (age, sex-ratio, marital status, etc.) as a whole.

name: iavh_collectionMode
displayName: Method used to collect the data; instrumentation characteristics (e.g., telephone interview, mail questionnaire, or other).

name: iavh_researchInstrument
displayName: Type of data collection instrument used. Structured indicates an instrument in which all respondents are asked the same questions/tests, possibly with precoded answers. If a small portion of such a questionnaire includes open-ended questions, provide appropriate comments. Semi-structured indicates that the research instrument contains mainly open-ended questions. Unstructured indicates that in-depth interviews were conducted.

name: iavh_dataCollectionSituation
displayName: Description of noteworthy aspects of the data collection situation. Includes information on factors such as cooperativeness of respondents, duration of interviews, number of call backs, or similar.

name: iavh_actionsToMinimizeLoss
displayName: Summary of actions taken to minimize data loss. Include information on actions such as follow-up visits, supervisory checks, historical matching, estimation, and so on.

name: iavh_controlOperations
displayName: Control OperationsMethods to facilitate data control performed by the primary investigator or by the data archive.

name: iavh_weighting
displayName: The use of sampling procedures might make it necessary to apply weights to produce accurate statistical results. Describes the criteria for using weights in analysis of a collection. If a weighting formula or coefficient was developed, the formula is provided, its elements are defined, and it is indicated how the formula was applied to the data.

name: iavh_cleaningOperations
displayName: Methods used to clean the data collection, such as consistency checking, wildcode checking, or other.

name: iavh_datasetLevelErrorNotes
displayName: Note element used for any information annotating or clarifying the methodology and processing of the study.

name: iavh_responseRate
displayName: Percentage of sample members who provided information.

name: iavh_samplingErrorEstimates
displayName: Measure of how precisely one can estimate a population value from a given sample.

name: iavh_otherDataAppraisal
displayName: Other issues pertaining to the data appraisal. Describe issues such as response variance, nonresponse rate  and testing for bias, interviewer and response bias, confidence levels, question bias, or similar.

name: iavh_socialScienceNotes
displayName: General notes about this Dataset.

name: iavh_socialScienceNotesType
displayName: Type of note.

name: iavh_socialScienceNotesSubject
displayName: Note subject.

name: iavh_socialScienceNotesText
displayName: Text for this note.

name: iavh_socialScienceBiases
displayName: Description of potential biases for the dataset

name: iavh_socioEcologicalTemplate
displayName: Template used for organizing this dataset

name: DatasetField
displayName: identifier

name: iavh_collectionMode
dataverseAlias: Personal interview on a paper form

name: iavh_collectionMode
dataverseAlias: Auto-filled form sent on webpage with closed answers

name: iavh_collectionMode
dataverseAlias: Auto-filled form sent on webpage with free answers
